agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond Council to explore scholarship funding for local students
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will receive a presentation from Richmond Promise, a local organization that helps Richmond students access and complete post-secondary education (including college, trade school, and university). After the presentation, Council will consider directing staff to explore funding options to support scholarships for Richmond youth. This is a proposed step — no funding has been approved yet.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
Q.2
Presentation from Richmond Promise: Helping to build a stronger Richmond by deeply supporting Richmond students to access and successfully complete post-secondary education (not only 4 year college)
•  RECEIVE a presentation from Richmond Promise on the organization’s current scope of services, how it supports youth before, during, and after post-secondary education, and the impact it has achieved to date and to highlight the program’s current needs as it continues to serve Richmond students; and DIRECT staff to explore funding to support scholarships for Richmond youth - Vice Mayor Doria Robinson (510-620-6568).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond Council to vote on opposing CO2 pipeline project
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider adopting a resolution that opposes the proposed Montezuma Carbon Sequestration Hub. This project would involve building a large network of CO2 pipelines through Richmond, Contra Costa, and Solano Counties to transport and store carbon waste underground. Councilmember Claudia Jimenez has put forward the resolution, citing risks to the health and safety of Richmond residents. The item is up for public comment and council deliberation — it has not yet been approved.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Attend the council meeting or submit public comment to voice your support or concerns before the vote.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
Q.1
Opposition to Proposed CO2 Waste Dumping Projects & CO2 Pipelines in Richmond, Contra Costa, and Solano Counties
RECEIVE a presentation on proposed Montezuma CO2 Hub, a carbon waste dumping project, that would require a massive build out of pipelines through Richmond, risking the health and safety of Richmond residents and ADOPT a resolution that opposes the proposed Montezuma Carbon Sequestration Hub's CO2 pipeline waste dumping projects and CO2 pipelines in Richmond, Contra Costa, and Solano Counties - Councilmember Claudia Jimenez (510-620-6565). This item was continued from the April 28, 2026, and May 5, 2026, meeting. THE PRESENTATION FOR THIS ITEM WAS RECEIVED ON MAY 5, 2026. THIS ITEM IS FOR PUBLIC COMMENT AND COUNCIL DELIBERATION ONLY.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to hold public hearing on city vacancies and recruitment efforts
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to hold a public hearing and receive a presentation on the status of city vacancies and the city's recruitment and retention efforts in 2025. This is a proposed annual public hearing — it has not yet been approved or enacted. The hearing will allow the public to learn about staffing gaps and how the city plans to attract and keep employees.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Attend the public hearing to learn about city staffing issues and share your input.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
P.4
Annual Public Hearing on Vacancies
•  HOLD a public hearing and RECEIVE a presentation on the status of vacancies and the City’s recruitment and retention efforts in 2025 – Human Resources (Nickie Mastay/Sharrone Taylor 510-620-6803).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on new Master Fee Schedule
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to hold a public hearing and consider a proposed ordinance (a new local law) that would update the Master Fee Schedule. This would establish new fees for various city services and permits. The proposal is up for a first reading — the first step in the approval process — and has not yet been passed. If approved, the new fees could affect the cost of business licences, permits, and other city services for local businesses.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Attend the public hearing to voice your concerns about proposed fee increases.

key_dates.label: Richmond Master Fee Schedule public hearing

agenda_briefs.source_body:
P.3
Master Fee Schedule Update
•  HOLD a public hearing; and INTRODUCE an ordinance (first reading) establishing certain fees for the proposed Master Fee Schedule – Finance Department (Emily Combs/Mubeen Qader/Antonio Banuelos 510-620-6741).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond to vote on special assessments for unpaid fines
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond Council is set to vote on a proposal to levy special assessments against properties with unpaid administrative citation fees and nuisance abatement costs. A special assessment is a charge added to a property's tax bill to recover money owed. If approved, the city would hold a public hearing first, then adopt a resolution to collect these unpaid invoices through property taxes. This affects property owners who have not paid fines for code violations or cleanup costs ordered by the city.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Attend the public hearing to voice concerns if you own a property with unpaid city fines or nuisance abatement costs.

key_dates.label: Public hearing on special assessments for unpaid fines

agenda_briefs.source_body:
P.2
Levy of Special Assessments for Unpaid Administrative Citation and Nuisance Abatement Costs
•  HOLD a public hearing; and ADOPT a resolution authorizing the levy of special assessments against certain properties with unpaid invoices related to administrative citation fees, and nuisance abatement costs pursuant to Richmond Municipal Code Chapters 2.62, 6.38, 9.22, and 11.76 – Community Development Department/Code Enforcement Division (Lina Velasco/ Eva Mann 510-621-1279).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond zoning and land-use changes to match state housing law
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on proposed changes to the city's zoning rules and General Plan. These changes are meant to bring local rules in line with recent updates to state housing law. The proposal would amend several sections of the Richmond Municipal Code (the city's official set of local laws) and the General Plan 2030 Land Use and Urban Design Element (the city's long-term plan for how land can be used). A public hearing will be held before the vote. If approved, these changes could affect what types of housing and buildings are allowed in different parts of the city.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Attend the public hearing to voice your support or concerns about the proposed zoning changes.

key_dates.label: Public hearing on proposed Richmond zoning and housing element changes

agenda_briefs.source_body:
P.1
General Plan and Zoning Text Amendments Related to 6th Cycle Housing Element Program Implementation
•  HOLD a public hearing; INTRODUCE (first reading) an ordinance, as recommended by Richmond’s Planning Commission, amending Articles 15.04.104, 15.04.201, 15.04.202, 15.04.203, 15.04.602, 15.04.607; Sections 15.04.610.180, 15.04.610.360, 15.04.805.040 and 15.04.806.040 of the Richmond Municipal Code (RMC); and ADOPT a resolution, as recommended by Richmond’s Planning Commission, amending the General Plan 2030 Land Use and Urban Design Element, to implement certain programs identified in the City’s adopted 6th Cycle Housing Element to align with recent changes to State Law - Community Development Department (Lina Velasco/Avery Stark 510-620-6714).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to review draft 2026-27 budget for key city services
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on the draft Fiscal Year 2026-27 Annual Operating Budget and the 2026-31 Five-Year Capital Improvement Plan. As part of the review, council will receive presentations from the Community Services, Economic Development, and Fire Departments. This is a proposed budget that has not yet been approved. The presentations will outline planned spending and projects for these departments. A final decision on the budget will come after all departmental presentations are complete and council discusses them.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the final council decision on the budget — it could affect city fees, permits, and services your business relies on.
agenda_briefs.industries: Professional Services, Construction & Development, Food Service
agenda_briefs.source_body:
O.1
Fiscal Year 2026-27 Draft Budget Department Presentations and Budget Updates
•  RECEIVE an update on departmental presentations from Community Services, Economic Development, and Fire Departments as part of the continued review of the draft Fiscal Year (FY) 2026-27 Annual Operating Budget and FY 2026-31 FiveYear Capital Improvement Plan, with discussions upon completion of all departmental presentations; and RECEIVE updated financial and operational considerations – City Manager’s Office/Finance Department (Shasa Curl/Emily Combs/Mubeen Qader 510-412-2077).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on updated sidewalk vendor rules
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed update to the Sidewalk Vendor Ordinance (Chapter 7.42 of the Richmond Municipal Code). The changes would update administrative procedures, waiver provisions, and enforcement mechanisms for sidewalk vendors. This item was first introduced at the April 28, 2026, and May 5, 2026, meetings and is now up for a first reading vote. If approved, it would change how vendors apply for permits, how waivers are granted, and how rules are enforced. The proposal has not been approved yet — council will consider it at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision date — this could affect your sidewalk vending licence or permit.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.13.f
Introduce Ordinance Amending Sidewalk Vendor Regulations (RMC Chapter 7.42)
INTRODUCE an ordinance (first reading) amending Chapter 7.42 of the Richmond Municipal Code (Sidewalk Vendor Ordinance) to update administrative procedures, waiver provisions, and enforcement mechanisms - Public Works Department (Andy Cho 620-6547/Robert Armijo 620-5477). This item was continued from the April 28, 2026, and May 5, 2026, meeting.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on $390,000 e-bike share contract extension
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on a proposed third contract amendment with Charleston Mobility for the citywide electric bikeshare program. If approved, the amendment would add $390,000 to the contract, bringing the total to $3.54 million. This would fund the program through the end of the current fiscal year while the city decides the program's long-term future during the 2026-27 budget process. The money would come from the Environmental and Community Investment Agreement (ECIA) Transportation budget. This item was previously discussed at council meetings on April 28 and May 5, 2026.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect bike-share availability for your customers or employees.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.13.e
Contract Amendment No. 3 with Charleston Mobility for Electric Bikeshare Operations
APPROVE a third contract amendment with Charleston Mobility in an amount not to exceed $390,000 for a total not-to-exceed contract amount of $3,540,341, to support the continued operation of the citywide electric bikeshare program till the end of the fiscal year to allow time to determine the future of the program during the Fiscal Year 2026-27 budget process; and APPROPRIATE $390,000 from the Environmental and Community Investment Agreement (ECIA) Transportation budget to cover the cost of the contract amendment – Public Works Department/Community Services Department (Gabino Arredondo 510-620-6606/LaShonda White 510-620-6828).​ This item was continued from the April 28, 2026, and May 5, 2026, meeting.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: $1.28M for Santa Fe Avenue slope stabilization in Richmond
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a proposal to fund the Santa Fe Avenue Slope Stabilization Project with $1.28 million. The money would come from three sources: $72,623.62 transferred from the Santa Rita Road Stabilization Project, $205,502.81 from the Quick-Build project (Cutting/Hoffman Project), and $1,000,000 from the Insurance Reserve Fund. If approved, the funds would pay for design, geotechnical services, and construction. This is a proposed decision — it has not been approved yet.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.13.d
Appropriate Funds for the Santa Fe Avenue Slope Stabilization Project
•  ADOPT a resolution to RATIFY the transfer of $72,623.62 from the Santa Rita Road Stabilization Project; APPROPRIATE $205,502.81 from the Quick-Build project funded through California’s Active Transportation Program for Cutting/Hoffman Project to fund design and geotechnical services; and APPROPRIATE $1,000,000 from the Insurance Reserve Fund to the General Capital Fund in the Fiscal Year 2026–27 budget for construction of the Santa Fe Avenue Slope Stabilization Project - Public Works Department (Hillal Hamdan 510-621-1612/ Daniel Chavarria 510-620-5478).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on $3.25M road repair fund list
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a resolution that would approve a list of road maintenance and rehabilitation projects for the 2026–27 fiscal year. If passed, the resolution would also set aside $3,251,983 from the Senate Bill 1 Road Maintenance and Rehabilitation Account (a special state fund for fixing roads) for the Public Works Department to carry out the work. This is a proposed decision — it has not been approved yet.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.13.c
Fiscal Year 2026–27 Senate Bill 1 Road Maintenance and Rehabilitation Fund Project List
•  ADOPT a resolution approving the Fiscal Year 2026–27 Senate Bill (SB) 1 Road Maintenance and Rehabilitation Account (RMRA) project list and APPROPRIATE $3,251,983 in RMRA funds to SB 1 Fund 1019 - Public Works Department (Hillal Hamdan 510-621-1612/Robert Armijo 510-620-5477).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond Council to vote on Complete Streets funding plan
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond Council is set to vote on a proposed funding allocation plan for Complete Streets Improvement projects. These projects include pavement management and streetscape improvements (like sidewalks, crosswalks, and street furniture). If approved, the plan would decide how money is spent on making streets safer and more accessible for everyone — drivers, pedestrians, and cyclists. The decision has not been made yet; Council will consider it at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date — if approved, this plan could affect local road work and business access.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.13.b
Complete Streets Improvement Elements Project Funding Allocation Plan
•  APPROVE the Complete Streets Improvements (Pavement Management, Streetscape Improvements, etc.) funding allocation plan – Public Works Department (Josef Munoz 510-620-6671/Daniel Chavarria 510-620-5478).

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on accepting Lincoln Elementary Safe Routes project
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on a resolution to formally accept the Lincoln Elementary School Safe Routes to School Improvement Project as complete. If approved, the city clerk would be authorized to ratify the previously recorded Notice of Completion (a formal document stating the project is finished), and the city engineer would be authorized to release applicable bonds and retention (money held back to ensure the work was done properly). This is a proposed decision that has not yet been enacted.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.13.a
Acceptance of the Lincoln Elementary School Safe Routes to School Improvement Project
•  ADOPT a resolution to ACCEPT the Lincoln Elementary School Safe Routes to School Improvement Project as complete; AUTHORIZE the city clerk to ratify the previously recorded Notice of Completion; and AUTHORIZE the city engineer to execute and release applicable bonds and retention – Public Works Department (Hillal Hamdan 621-1612/Daniel Chavarria 620-5478).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on $7.2M animal control contract
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving a contract with Contra Costa County Animal Services Department worth up to $7.2 million. This would cover animal control services for the 2026–2027 fiscal year, plus a possible three-year extension through June 2030. The item was carried over from the May 5, 2026 meeting and is up for a vote at the next council meeting. If approved, the contract would run from July 1, 2022 to June 30, 2030 — meaning it would also cover past services already provided.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action needed from business owners at this time — this is a routine municipal service contract.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.12.c
Agreement with Contra Costa County Animal Services Department for Ongoing Animal Control Services
•  APPROVE and AUTHORIZE a contract with Contra Costa County Animal Services Department in an amount not to exceed $7,200,000 for the continuation of Animal Control Services for Fiscal Year 2026–2027, with an additional three-year term for Fiscal Years 2027–2028, 2028–2029, and 2029–2030, for a total contract term of July 1, 2022, through June 30, 2030 – Police Department (Chief Timothy Simmons 510-621-1802). This item was continued from the May 5, 2026, meeting.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on $75,000 gun range rental contract
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed three-year contract with the Richmond Rod and Gun Club for shooting range facility rentals. If approved, the contract would cost up to $25,000 per year (for a total of $75,000) and run from July 1, 2026, through June 30, 2029. The contract also includes two optional one-year extensions at the same annual rate. This is a 'sole-source' contract, meaning the city would hire this one club without seeking bids from other companies. The Police Department is recommending this deal for officer training and other law enforcement needs.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action needed from business owners — this is an internal city contract for police training.

key_dates.label: Council vote on proposed Richmond Rod and Gun Club contract

agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.12.b
Contract with Richmond Rod and Gun Club for Shooting Range Facility Rental
•  APPROVE a three-year sole-source contract with Richmond Rod and Gun Club for shooting range facility rentals in an amount not to exceed $25,000 annually, for a total not to exceed $75,000, for the term July 1, 2026, through June 30, 2029, with two one-year extension options at $25,000 per year – Police Department (Chief Timothy Simmons 510-621-1802).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Verizon Connect GPS tracking contract for Richmond police vehicles up to $150,000
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving a three-year contract with Verizon Connect to provide GPS tracking and management services for Richmond Police Department vehicles. The proposed contract would cost up to $150,000 over three years, starting July 1, 2026, with an option to extend for two additional one-year periods at up to $50,000 per year. This is a proposed decision that has not yet been approved.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action required from business owners at this time — this is an internal police department operational contract.

key_dates.label: Proposed contract start date — Richmond police GPS tracking
Proposed contract end date — Richmond police GPS tracking

agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.12.a
Contract with Verizon Connect for Police Vehicle GPS Service
•  APPROVE a contract with Verizon Connect for monthly GPS tracking and management in an amount not to exceed $150,000 over a three-year term from July 1, 2026, to June 30, 2029, plus two one-year extension options not to exceed $50,000 per year – Police Department (Chief Timothy Simmons 510-621-1802).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Four new stakeholder seats on Macdonald Avenue Corridor Task Force
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider adding four new stakeholder positions to the Macdonald Avenue Corridor Task Force. If approved, these seats would be filled through the standard boards and commissions process. This is a proposed change that has not yet been voted on — it is up for approval at an upcoming council meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.11.e
Expansion of the Macdonald Avenue Corridor Task Force Membership
•  APPROVE the addition of four (4) new stakeholder positions to the Macdonald Avenue Corridor Task Force which will be filled through the standard boards and commissions process—Mayor’s Office (Eduardo Martinez (510-620-6502).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council committee to rewrite meeting rules
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a request to create a special committee (called an 'ad-hoc committee') that would be tasked with rewriting the City Council Rules of Procedure and Order. The goal is to make council meetings run more smoothly and efficiently. If approved, the mayor would appoint members to this committee, and they would have 60 days to propose changes for the full council to vote on. This is a proposed step — no rules have been changed yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action needed now. Watch for the council decision on whether to create this committee.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.10.a
Ad-Hoc Committee to Amend City Council Rules of Procedure and Order
•  REQUEST that Mayor Eduardo Martinez appoint a City Council ad hoc committee to work with the city clerk, city attorney, and city manager to develop proposed amendments to the City Council Rules of Procedure and Order and other operational refinements to improve efficiency, for consideration by the full City Council within 60 days of the committee’s appointment - City Clerk’s Office/City Attorney’s Office/City Manager’s Office (Pamela Christian 510-620-6513/Shannon Moore 510-620-6509/Shasa Curl 510-620-6512).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: 5% salary adjustment for Richmond City Clerk in 2026
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving a 5% salary increase for the City Clerk, effective March 2, 2026. This proposed adjustment is part of the existing agreement with the IFPTE Local 21 union for executive management staff. The decision has not been made yet — it is up for approval at an upcoming council meeting.

key_dates.label: Proposed City Clerk salary increase takes effect

agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.9.a
Annual Compensation Adjustment for the City Clerk
•  APPROVE a five percent (5%) salary adjustment for the city clerk effective March 2, 2026, in accordance with the IFPTE Local 21 Executive Management Memorandum of Understanding Section 6.4D – Human Resources Department (Nickie Mastay/Sharrone Taylor 510-620-6602).

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on wildfire preparedness committee recommendations
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to consider recommendations from the Wildfire Preparedness Ad Hoc Committee to improve the city's wildfire preparedness and mitigation efforts. Council will provide direction to staff on which recommendation to pursue during the budget checklist on proposed spending. The committee will be disbanded after this decision. This is a proposed decision — it has not been approved yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — it could affect property owners and businesses in wildfire-prone areas.
agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Natural Resources
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.8.c
Wildfire Preparedness Ad Hoc Committee Recommendations
•  RECEIVE a written presentation from the Wildfire Preparedness Ad Hoc Committee; PROVIDE DIRECTION to staff on the City Council’s preferred recommendation to improve the City’s wildfire preparedness and mitigation efforts during the budget checklist on proposed appropriations; and ACKNOWLEDGE the disbanding of the Wildfire Preparedness Ad Hoc Committee – Wildfire Preparedness Ad Hoc Committee (Chief Aaron Osorio 510-307-8021).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on EMS agreement extension with Contra Costa
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed amendment to the existing interagency agreement with Contra Costa County Health Services for the Emergency Medical Services (EMS) Program. If approved, the agreement would be extended through June 30, 2027, and an additional $229,462 in Measure H funding would be accepted, bringing the total contract payment limit to $458,924. This is a proposed decision that has not yet been enacted.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action required from business owners at this time; monitor council decision for potential impact on local emergency services.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.8.b
Interagency Agreement Amendment with Contra Costa County Health Services for Emergency Medical Services Program
•  ADOPT a resolution to: (1) APPROVE and AUTHORIZE the city manager, or designee to execute an amendment to the agreement with Contra Costa County Health Services for Emergency Medical Services Program, extending the term of the current interagency agreement through June 30, 2027; and (2) ACCEPT and APPROPRIATE funds in an amount not to exceed an additional $229,462 in Measure H funding from the Contra Costa County Health Services, bringing the total contract payment limit to $458,924 – Fire Department (Chief Aaron Osorio 510-307-8021/Deputy Chief Rico Rincon 510-307-8041).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to designate officials for disaster aid applications
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a resolution that would authorize specific city officials to apply for state and federal disaster assistance. If approved, the Fire Department officials named in the resolution would be allowed to submit applications to the California Governor’s Office of Emergency Services for financial help under the California Disaster Assistance Act and the federal Robert T. Stafford Disaster Relief and Emergency Assistance Act. This is a proposed step to speed up getting disaster funds if needed — it has not been approved yet.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.8.a
Designating Officials Authorized to Execute and Submit Application for Federal and State Financial Disaster Assistance
•  ADOPT a resolution designating officials that are authorized to execute and submit applications to the State of California Governor’s Office of Emergency Services for state financial assistance under the California Disaster Assistance Act and the federal Robert T. Stafford Disaster Relief and Emergency Assistance Act – Fire Department (Shasa Curl 510-620-6512/Emily Combs 510-620-6740/Chief Aaron Osorio 510-307-8021).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond Donation Acceptance Policy up for council vote
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on a proposed Donation Acceptance Policy. If approved, this policy would create formal rules for how the city accepts gifts, donations, and bequests. The policy is meant to ensure donations are handled consistently and transparently. Council will consider adopting the policy at an upcoming meeting. This is not yet in effect — it is a proposal under review.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.7.c
Donation Acceptance Policy
•  ADOPT a resolution approving the City of Richmond Donation Acceptance Policy – Finance Department (Emily Combs/Jerry Chak 510-620-6569).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond pension tax override rate set at 0.14% for 2026-27
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on a proposed ordinance that would set the tax rate for the Tax Override Pension Fund at 0.14% for the 2026-27 fiscal year. This is the first reading of the ordinance, meaning it has been introduced but not yet approved. If passed, this rate would apply to property taxes to help fund the city's pension obligations. The Finance Department is recommending this rate.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this proposed rate could affect your property taxes if you own a business in Richmond.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.7.b
Setting Tax Rate for the Pension Tax Override Fund for Fiscal Year 2026-27
•  INTRODUCE an ordinance (first reading) setting the tax rate for the Tax Override Pension Fund for Fiscal Year 2026-2027 at 0.14 percent – Finance Department (Emily Combs/Mubeen Qader/ Antonio Banuelos 510-620-6741).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond Council to write off $535,499 in uncollected debts
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond Council is set to vote on a proposal to write off $535,499.12 in unpaid accounts receivable. 'Write-off' means the city would officially give up trying to collect these debts and remove them from its books. This does not cancel the debt — the city could still try to collect in the future — but it is a signal that the city considers these amounts unlikely to be paid. The proposal covers delinquent (overdue) and uncollectible accounts from various city services. A decision is expected at the next council meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action required from business owners at this time. Watch for the council decision — it may signal tighter future collection practices.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.7.a
Approval of Write-Off of Uncollectible Accounts Receivables
•  ADOPT a resolution authorizing the write-off of delinquent and/or uncollectible accounts receivable in the amount of $535,499.12 – Finance Department (Emily Combs/Mubeen Qader/Antonio Banuelos 510-620-6741).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on $60K facade grant program boost
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on a proposed contract amendment with the Renaissance Entrepreneurship Center. If approved, the contract would increase by $60,000 (to a total of $400,000) and extend the program through June 30, 2027. This money would help pay for the Small Business Beautification Facade Improvement Program, which gives grants to eligible local businesses to fix up their storefronts. The decision has not been made yet — council will consider it at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, eligible Richmond small businesses can apply for facade improvement grants.

key_dates.label: Council vote on facade program contract amendment

agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.6.b
Contract Amendment with Renaissance Entrepreneurship Center for Small Business Beautification Pilot Program
•  APPROVE the first amendment to the contract with Renaissance Entrepreneurship Center to increase the total contract amount by $60,000 for a revised amount not to exceed $400,000, extend the term through June 30, 2027, and support payment administration for eligible businesses participating in the Small Business Beautification Facade Improvement Program - Economic Development Department (Jesson de Leon 510-412-2023/Junne Garcia 510-620-6524/Nannette Beacham 510-620-1306).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Downtown Richmond property assessment levy for 2026-2027
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a resolution that would allow the City of Richmond to collect a special levy (a mandatory fee) from property owners in the Downtown Richmond Property and Business Improvement District. If approved, this levy would be added to the property tax bills for the 2026-2027 fiscal year. The money would be used to fund improvements and services in the downtown area.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — if approved, this levy will appear on your property tax bill.
agenda_briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail, Food Service
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.6.a
Downtown Richmond Property and Business Improvement District Fiscal Year 2026–2027 Assessment Levy
•  ADOPT a resolution authorizing the City of Richmond to levy assessments on property owners subject to the Downtown Richmond Property and Business Improvement District through the County of Contra Costa property tax rolls for Fiscal Year 2026–2027 – Economic Development Department (Jesson de Leon 510-412-2023/Antonio Banuelos 510-620-6741/Junne Garcia 510-620-6524/Nannette Beacham 510-620-1306).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond Council to accept $35,000 Chevron grant for street outreach
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond Council is set to vote on a resolution to accept a $35,000 grant from the Chevron Community Engagement Foundation. If approved, the money would go to the Office of Neighborhood Safety (ONS) to fund street outreach and mobile crisis response services. This is a proposed decision — it has not been approved yet and will be decided at an upcoming council meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action needed from business owners at this time — watch for the council decision.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.5.a
Grant Award from Chevron Community Engagement Foundation
•  ADOPT a resolution to ACCEPT and APPROPRIATE a $35,000 grant award from the Chevron Community Engagement Foundation for the Office of Neighborhood Safety (ONS) to Enhance Street Outreach/Mobile Crises Response – Community Services Department (LaShonda White/Sam Vaughn 510-620-5404).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond Council to vote on $50,000 housing program support contract
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond Council is set to vote on a proposed contract with Makanai, LLC to provide budget and reporting support for past housing programs. If approved, the contract would be for up to $50,000 over a three-year term, with the option to extend for one additional year. This is an upcoming decision — it has not been approved yet. The contract would help the Community Development Department with financial tracking and reporting for housing programs that have already been completed.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, this contract could affect how past housing program funds are tracked and reported.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.4.b
Contract with Makanai, LLC for Budget and Reporting Support for Housing Programs
•  APPROVE an on-call contract with Makanai, LLC to provide budget and reporting support related to past housing programs in an amount not to exceed $50,000, for a three-year term with a term expiration of June 30, 2029, with one one-year extension option – Community Development Department (Lina Velasco/Jesus Morales 510-620-6706).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on $300K hearing officer contracts
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on a proposal to hire two law firms, Nastich Law and Robert M. Snider, Attorney at Law, as on-call administrative hearing officers. If approved, each firm could be paid up to $300,000 over three years to handle hearings for things like bylaw disputes or licence appeals. This is not a final decision yet — council will consider it at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.4.a
On-Call Contracts with Nastich Law and Robert M. Snider, Attorney at Law for Administrative Hearing Officer Services
•  ADOPT a resolution authorizing on-call contracts with Nastich Law and Robert M. Snider, Attorney at Law, to provide Administrative Hearing Officer services, for an amount not to exceed $300,000 per firm, for a three-year term - Community Development Department (Lina Velasco/ Eva Mann 510-621-1283).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Fee waiver for WCCUSD Latino/x graduation at Richmond Auditorium
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a proposal to waive all fees, staffing costs, and other expenses for the West Contra Costa Unified School District's 2026 Latino/x Graduation Ceremony at the Richmond Memorial Auditorium. If approved, the city manager would be authorized to spend city resources — including potential overtime for Police, Fire, Public Works, and Community Services departments — to support the event. This is a proposed decision that has not yet been voted on.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — if approved, this could affect city staffing and resource allocation for the event.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.3.a
Waiver of Auditorium Fees, Staffing Costs, and Other Associated Costs to Support the West Contra Costa Unified School District Latino/x Graduation at the Richmond Memorial Auditorium and Convention Center
•  REQUEST the city manager issue fee waivers to West Contra Costa Unified School District for the use of the Richmond Memorial Auditorium in 2026 for the Latino/x Graduation Ceremony or Ceremonies, and AUTHORIZE the city manager or their designee to expend City resources to support the graduation, which may include overtime from Police, Fire, Public Works, Community Services, and other departments as required  - Councilmember Claudia Jimenez (510-620-6565).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on $110,000 legal research contract
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving a three-year contract with Thomson Reuters for legal research subscription services, including Westlaw and CoCounsel Legal. The contract would cost up to $110,000 and run from July 1, 2026, through June 30, 2029. This is a proposed decision — it has not been approved yet.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.1.c
Contract with Thomson Reuters Enterprise for Legal Research Subscription Services
•  APPROVE a contract with Thomson Reuters for legal research subscription services for Westlaw and CoCounsel Legal in an amount not to exceed $110,000 for the term of July 1, 2026, through June 30, 2029, subject to non-substantive revisions approved by the City Attorney’s Office; and AUTHORIZE the city manager or their designee to execute the contract – City Attorney’s Office (Christopher Dykzeul 510-620-6507/Shannon Moore 510-620-6509).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on $150K contract for confidential HR probes
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving a three-year agreement with Ellis Investigations to conduct confidential personnel investigations for the city. The proposed contract would run from July 1, 2026, through June 30, 2029, and would not exceed $150,000 total. This is a legal services agreement for internal HR matters — it covers things like employee misconduct or workplace complaints that need an outside investigator. The decision has not been made yet; it is up for approval at an upcoming council meeting.

key_dates.label: Proposed Ellis Investigations contract start date — if approved
Proposed Ellis Investigations contract end date — if approved

agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.1.b
Legal Services Agreement with Ellis Investigations for Confidential Personnel Investigations
•  APPROVE a legal services agreement with Ellis Investigations to conduct confidential personnel investigations for a term of July 1, 2026, through June 30, 2029, for a total amount not to exceed $150,000 – City Attorney’s Office/Human Resources Department (Shannon Moore/Nickie Mastay/Sharrone Taylor 510-620-6509).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on $75K increase for Winehaven legal fees
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving a fourth amendment to the legal services agreement with the law firm Nevers, Palazzo, Packard, Wildermuth & Wynner, PC, for legal work related to the Winehaven Legacy lawsuit and related matters. The proposed change would increase the total contract amount by $75,000 — bringing it to a maximum of $450,000 — and extend the agreement through December 31, 2027. The $75,000 would come from the city's General Fund. This is a proposed decision and has not yet been approved.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.1.a
Legal Services Agreement No. 4 with Nevers, Palazzo, Packard, Wildermuth & Wynner, PC
•  APPROVE a fourth amendment to the legal services agreement with Nevers, Palazzo, Packard, Wildermuth & Wynner, PC, for legal representation related to the Winehaven Legacy litigation and related matters, to increase compensation by $75,000 for a total contract amount not to exceed $450,000, extend the term through December 31, 2027, and APPROPRIATE $75,000 from the General Fund to cover associated legal fees. – City Attorney’s Office (Kimberly Chin 510-620-6709/Shannon Moore 510-620-6505).
